reasoning: The informal statement says a group of order 200 has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup. The Lean statement assumes `[Fintype G] [Group G]` with `card G = 200` (which faithfully encodes "group of order 200") and concludes `∃ N : Sylow 5 G, N.Normal`. In Mathlib, `Sylow 5 G` is the type of Sylow 5-subgroups, and `N.Normal` is the standard normal subgroup property on the underlying subgroup. The existential "there exists a normal Sylow 5-subgroup" is the natural reading of "has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup." Since in a finite group Sylow p-subgroups are all conjugate, having one normal is equivalent to uniqueness/all being normal, so there is no semantic gap. The formalization is strictly faithful.
